tool.name: GaCDI Manifest Builder (GDC)
tool.description: build a filtered GDC download manifest and enriched metadata table
tool.command: gacdi-manifest gdc #if str($filters.project).strip() != '' --project '$filters.project' #end if #if str($filters.data_category) != '' --data-category '$filters.data_category' #end if #if str($filters.data_type).strip() != '' --data-type '$filters.data_type' #end if #if str($filters.experimental_strategy) != '' --experimental-strategy '$filters.experimental_strategy' #end if #if str($filters.data_format) != '' --data-format '$filters.data_format' #end if #if str($filters.access) != '' --access '$filters.access' #end if #if str($filters.sample_type).strip() != '' --sample-type '$filters.sample_type' #end if #for $ef in $extra_filters --extra-filter 'field=${ef.field};op=${ef.op};values=${ef.values}' #end for #if $advanced.raw_filters --raw-filters '$advanced.raw_filters' #end if #if $count_only --count-only #else #if str($max_files) != '' --max-files $max_files #end if #end if #if $enrich.enable == 'yes' #if str($enrich.cbioportal_study).strip() != '' --cbioportal-study '$enrich.cbioportal_study' #if str($enrich.cbioportal_attrs).strip() != '' --cbioportal-attrs '$enrich.cbioportal_attrs' #end if #end if #if $enrich.annotation_tsv --annotation-tsv '$enrich.annotation_tsv' --annotation-key-col '$enrich.annotation_key_col' #end if #end if --join-level '$join_level' #if not $trim_vial --no-trim-vial #end if --manifest-out '$manifest' --metadata-out '$metadata' --report-out '$report'

param.name: data_type
param.label: Data type
param.help: e.g. Slide Image, Gene Expression Quantification, Aligned Reads.

param.name: sample_type
param.label: Sample type
param.help: e.g. Primary Tumor, Solid Tissue Normal.

repeat.name: extra_filters
repeat.title: Custom filter
repeat.help: Add filters for any GDC field not covered above.

param.name: raw_filters
param.label: Raw GDC filters JSON
param.help: A full GDC 'filters' object; merged (AND) with the fields above.

param.name: cbioportal_study
param.label: cBioPortal study id
param.help: e.g. brca_tcga_pan_can_atlas_2018. Leave blank to skip cBioPortal.

param.name: annotation_tsv
param.label: Annotation table (TSV)
param.help: Optional. Extra per-sample annotations (e.g. histology) merged by barcode.

param.name: join_level
param.label: Join level
param.help: Granularity for matching file barcodes to annotations.

param.name: trim_vial
param.label: Trim vial letter when matching
param.help: Treat 01A and 01 as the same sample.

param.name: max_files
param.label: Maximum number of files
param.help: Cap the manifest size. Leave blank for no limit.

param.name: count_only
param.label: Preview counts only
param.help: Report how many files match (with facet breakdown) without building the full manifest.

tool.help:
**GaCDI Manifest Builder (GDC)**
Builds a **targeted** download manifest from the NCI `Genomic Data Commons <https://gdc.cancer.gov/>`_ by filtering, so you fetch exactly the files you want instead of a whole dataset. It produces three outputs:
1. **GDC manifest** — strict ``id / filename / md5 / size / state`` format, ready to feed straight into the **GaCDI GDC** importer or ``gdc-client``. 2. **metadata** — a research table joining the file records to sample barcodes and any clinical/molecular annotations you enable (GDC fields, cBioPortal subtypes such as PAM50 and ER/PR/HER2, and/or an uploaded annotation table). 3. **report** — match/precision QC: counts, unmatched files, unused annotations and colliding keys. In *Preview counts only* mode this holds the match count and a facet breakdown so you can refine filters cheaply before building.
**Why two files?** ``gdc-client`` rejects a manifest that contains extra clinical columns or lacks ``state`` — so the lean manifest is kept separate from the rich metadata table, which you join to the downloaded files afterwards by barcode.
**Filtering**
Use the guided facets for the common fields, add *Custom filter* rows for anything else, and/or supply a full **raw filters JSON** — all are AND-combined.
